Best Spreadsheet Software for Small Business (2026)

For most small businesses, Google Workspace (Sheets) is the best value for collaboration, while Microsoft 365 (Excel) wins for analysis and finance. Zoho and LibreOffice are strong budget picks. Here is how to choose for your team.

By The Sheetlift Team Published July 22, 2026 2 min read

The best spreadsheet software for a small business is the one your team will actually use together, at a price that fits. For most, that is Google Sheets (via Workspace) for its unbeatable collaboration; for finance-heavy work, Excel (via Microsoft 365) is worth the extra cost. Here is how the main options compare and who each suits.

The options at a glance

Spreadsheet software for small business, compared
SoftwarePrice modelBest for
Google Workspace (Sheets)Low monthly per userTeams that live in the browser and collaborate daily
Microsoft 365 (Excel)Monthly per userFinance, analysis, and Office-heavy workflows
Zoho Sheet / WorkplaceFree tier + cheap paidBudget-conscious teams wanting an all-in-one
LibreOffice CalcFreeOne-off use, tight budgets, no cloud needed
AirtableFree tier + paidTeams managing projects and data, not just numbers

Best overall for most small businesses: Google Workspace

Google Sheets, as part of Workspace, gives a small team real-time collaboration, automatic saving, easy sharing, and email plus storage in one low monthly price. There is almost nothing to manage and nothing to install. For the majority of small businesses, it is the most productive, lowest-friction choice.

Best for finance and analysis: Microsoft 365

If your business runs on serious modeling, reporting, or accounting, Excel's depth pays for itself. Power Query, Power Pivot, and its huge function set handle work Sheets struggles with. The full case is in our Excel vs Google Sheets comparison.

Best on a tight budget: Zoho and LibreOffice

If cost is the deciding factor, Zoho Sheet offers a generous free tier with real collaboration, and LibreOffice Calc is a completely free desktop app. Both appear in our wider list of best Google Sheets alternatives.

Tip: Match the tool to your bottleneck: if it is teamwork, choose Sheets; if it is analysis, choose Excel; if it is budget, choose Zoho or LibreOffice. Do not pay for power you will not use.

Frequently asked questions

What is the cheapest spreadsheet software for a small business?

LibreOffice Calc is completely free, and Google Sheets is free for individuals (small monthly cost per user under Workspace for business features). Zoho Sheet also has a strong free tier.

Should a small business use Excel or Google Sheets?

Most small businesses are better served by Google Sheets for its easy collaboration and low cost. Choose Excel if your work is finance-heavy or needs advanced analysis tools like Power Query.
